title: Understand: solar eclipses
description: Check your understanding of solar eclipses in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which diagram correctly shows the positions of the sun, Earth, and the moon during a solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The moon is shown between the sun and Earth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ba9b2d8b35a2928fdb606e5db5bda4c.svg)
![Earth is shown between the sun and the moon.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/75dddfd1dd912fcf33284f782d13f123.svg)
![Earth is shown to the right of the sun. The moon is shown above Earth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/21ba45ee9174d0fa9ad5d3bf50b9315f.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which diagram correctly shows the positions of the moon, Earth, and the sun during a solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The moon is shown between Earth and the sun.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/847bc3f7e3cd8df39e3576972cea06fe.svg)
![Earth is shown between the moon and the sun.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/85e600a458a00625de6c128da3f7174a.svg)
![Earth is shown to the left of the sun. The moon is shown below Earth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9882ec26cf4838183eb56b19ff0221ba.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which diagram correctly shows the positions of the sun, Earth, and the moon during a solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The moon is shown between the sun and Earth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2201b7995e1bdb2faf1b5d617f48dff3.svg)
![Earth is shown between the sun and the moon.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c39d1cacfd8128e23b538bf4b124435c.svg)
![Earth is shown to the right of the sun. The moon is shown below Earth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4fce7deeadb7a2bb73b67dd30235d03e.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Solar eclipses can occur during which moon phase(s)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a new moon only
a full moon only
both a new and a full moon

assessment_items.question_content:
**When can a solar eclipse occur?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: during a full moon only
during a new moon only
during both a full moon and a new moon

assessment_items.question_content:
**When can a solar eclipse occur?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: during a new moon only
during both a new moon and a full moon
during any phase of the moon

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the sun’s appearance during a total solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The sun’s corona and part of its disk are visible.
Only the sun’s corona is visible.
No part of the sun is visible.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the sun’s appearance during a total solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The sun’s disk is obscured, but its corona is visible.
The sun’s corona is obscured, but its disk is visible.
The sun’s disk and corona are both obscured.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the sun’s appearance during a total solar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The sun’s corona is covered, but its disk is visible.
The sun’s disk is covered, but its corona is visible.
The sun’s corona and disk are both covered.

assessment_items.question_content:
On average, the air temperature drops by $10$ degrees Fahrenheit during a total solar eclipse.

**Why does this happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: During a total solar eclipse, the moon blocks direct sunlight from reaching Earth.
During a total solar eclipse, the moon absorbs heat from Earth’s surface.
During a total solar eclipse, the sun temporarily goes below the horizon.

assessment_items.question_content:
On average, the air temperature drops by $10$ degrees Fahrenheit during a total solar eclipse.

**Why does this happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The sun is blocked by the moon during a total solar eclipse.
The sun stops producing energy during a total solar eclipse.
The sun temporarily sets during a total solar eclipse.

assessment_items.question_content:
On average, the air temperature drops by $10$ degrees Fahrenheit during a total solar eclipse.

**Why does this happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The moon blocks the sun’s light during a total solar eclipse.
The moon absorbs heat from Earth’s surface during a total solar eclipse.
The moon prevents the sun from producing energy during a total solar eclipse.
